rulings.Case Title: MARY WILLIAMS VS. MERCY HOUSING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alt Summ Adj; Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA X's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Continued to February 5, 2014. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copy of opposition to Department 501 no later than January 30, 2014 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. The court is in receipt of the courtesy copies of moving and reply papers. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALLYSYN OVERTON VS. PATRICK LAKE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT ALLYSYN OVERTON's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED without leave to amend. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA WONG VS. THE HARRISON TEAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT LINDA HARRISON and ELLEN ANDERSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to allege a cognizable cause of action in compliance with California Rules of Court pleading format rules. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. BONNIE CURRY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Records And Register Of Actions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANT BONNIE CURRY's Motion To Seal Records And Register Of Actions DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain burden. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ERLINDA SAN JUAN VS. MICHAEL CIOFALO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL CIOFALO and EVE SEGASTURE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Moving party shifted burden. Opposing party failed to create triable issue of fact. Even taking into consideration the full amount of the partial satisfaction of judgment and rent demanded in the "Three-Day Notice," the total sum is bellow the $88,932 judgment determined to be excepted from bankruptcy discharge. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ERLINDA SAN JUAN VS. JENNER E OCHOA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT BRENDA OCHOA, JACKELINE OCHOA and JENNER OCHOA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Moving party shifted burden. Opposing party failed to create triable issue of fact. Even taking into consideration the full amount of the partial satisfaction of judgment and rent demanded in the "Three-Day Notice," the total sum is bellow the $74,589 judgment determined to be excepted from bankruptcy discharge. = (501/REQ)
